Question: The special visceral afferent fibres of the facial nerve are located in which nuclei:

Options:
A: Motor nucleus  
B: Nucleus ambiguous  
C: Nucleus of tractus solitarius  
D:Lacrimary nucleus  

## Previous Discussion:
agent1: Agent1 concluded: Answer:

Answer: B: Nucleus ambiguous  

The special visceral afferent (SVA) fibers of the facial nerve, which carry sensory information from the external ear and taste from the anterior two-thirds of the tongue, synapse in the nucleus ambiguus.
